data.text: I’m reading the bead contract and the TD-037 design first, then I’ll map each acceptance criterion to concrete code and tests before touching anything.

  open:   ddx-51ac997b (escalation ladder.Next + tests)

  open:   ddx-2436fa73 (BuildReviewExecuteRequest + remove implHarness default)

  open:   ddx-0ca4ffda (routinglint guard)
  open:   ddx-5f1eac4f (FEAT-006/010/014 + ADR-021)

What is done: 1/9 child beads closed (the upstream Fizeau contract bead, which unblocks several others).

What is blocking: 8 child beads remain open. Per the planning round's locked decisions, those children carry the testable ACs; the epic is a tracker, not a unit of implementation. Bundling all of them into one attempt would violate the size-check guidance in the execute-bead instructions (multiple feature-sized pieces, >500 LOC across unrelated packages, AC mixes design/impl/tests/docs).

What a follow-up attempt would need: nothing at the epic level. The execute-loop should drain the children individually (most are tier:cheap or tier:standard; ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-2436fa73 unblocked by the closed B1, so they are ready). Once all children close, this epic can be closed administratively (or by re-running execute-bead, which will then find AC satisfied).

No code changes committed in this attempt.

.ddx/executions/20260506T210004-db8d7e99/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM
DDx has a partial tier-label mechanism, but durable bead-level execution hints are underspecified. Agents can currently copy route-shaped settings into bead metadata without a clear lint/audit contract, and smart-tier usage is not tied to a required justification.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:23-39 documents that `ddx try` infers tier from labels today, but arbitrary custom fields are preserved without a routing contract. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:62-92 requires smart justification, and lines 112-161 require durable route-pin rejection plus execution-routing-intent evidence.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: This bead defines and implements the durable execution-hint contract that later prose beads rely on. Weak implementation would let agents persist concrete model or harness pins into queue metadata, directly violating ADR-024.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add typed parsing for `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` labels.
- Extract and validate a `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` section for smart-tier beads.
- Add bead lifecycle lint findings for missing smart justification and durable route-pin fields/labels.
- Record `execution-routing-intent` evidence with source, requested tier/power, smart justification, actual route facts, and degradation notes.
- Extend agent metrics projection enough to audit smart-hint usage and rejected durable route pins.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add durable bead-level harness/provider/model/model-ref pins.
- Do not change Fizeau routing internals.
- Do not implement a materialized metrics rollup store.

DEPS
No deps. This must land before the Vale/prose child beads rely on tier hints.",
.ddx/executions/20260506T210004-db8d7e99/manifest.json:13:    "acceptance": "1. `TestExecutionHintParse_ValidTierLabels` covers `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` parsing.
2. `TestExecutionHintParse_SmartRequiresJustification` covers missing and present `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` text.
3. `TestExecutionHintLint_RejectsDurableRoutePins` covers forbidden fields/labels such as `execution-model` and `harness:claude`.
4. `TestTryRecordsExecutionRoutingIntent` proves `ddx try` evidence records source `bead_hint`, requested tier, smart justification, and actual route facts when available.
5. `TestAgentMetricsIncludesRoutingIntent` proves the normalized attempt projection can count smart-hinted attempts and rejected route pins.
6. Existing heuristic inference tests still pass when no tier hint exists.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/escalation/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/agentmetrics/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",

.ddx/beads.jsonl:274:{"acceptance":"1. `TestExecutionHintParse_ValidTierLabels` covers `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` parsing.
2. `TestExecutionHintParse_SmartRequiresJustification` covers missing and present `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` text.
3. `TestExecutionHintLint_RejectsDurableRoutePins` covers forbidden fields/labels such as `execution-model` and `harness:claude`.
4. `TestTryRecordsExecutionRoutingIntent` proves `ddx try` evidence records source `bead_hint`, requested tier, smart justification, and actual route facts when available.
5. `TestAgentMetricsIncludesRoutingIntent` proves the normalized attempt projection can count smart-hinted attempts and rejected route pins.
6. Existing heuristic inference tests still pass when no tier hint exists.

8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-06T21:22:28Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2049424","created_at":"2026-05-06T20:56:02.493376447Z","description":"PROBLEM
DDx has a partial tier-label mechanism, but durable bead-level execution hints are underspecified. Agents can currently copy route-shaped settings into bead metadata without a clear lint/audit contract, and smart-tier usage is not tied to a required justification.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:23-39 documents that `ddx try` infers tier from labels today, but arbitrary custom fields are preserved without a routing contract. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:62-92 requires smart justification, and lines 112-161 require durable route-pin rejection plus execution-routing-intent evidence.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: This bead defines and implements the durable execution-hint contract that later prose beads rely on. Weak implementation would let agents persist concrete model or harness pins into queue metadata, directly violating ADR-024.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add typed parsing for `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` labels.
- Extract and validate a `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` section for smart-tier beads.
- Add bead lifecycle lint findings for missing smart justification and durable route-pin fields/labels.
- Record `execution-routing-intent` evidence with source, requested tier/power, smart justification, actual route facts, and degradation notes.
- Extend agent metrics projection enough to audit smart-hint usage and rejected durable route pins.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add durable bead-level harness/provider/model/model-ref pins.
- Do not change Fizeau routing internals.
- Do not implement a materialized metrics rollup store.

DEPS
No deps. 6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-06T21:00:14.799114398Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-33acb746","depends_on_id":"ddx-4e9f610f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-06T21:00:21Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
After the Vale-backed checker, skill, and workflow hook work, DDx docs need one measured cleanup pass that applies high-signal prose findings and records the before/after result.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:361-369 says full-doc cleanup must wait until checker, skill, and workflow evidence paths are working. The cleanup needs to record finding count and word-count delta instead of silently rewriting docs.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Corpus-scale editorial cleanup is high-judgment. The agent must preserve DDx technical meaning, commands, paths, IDs, acceptance criteria, and rationale while reducing filler and AI-slop constructions where safe.

PROPOSED FIX
- Run the Vale-backed `ddx doc prose` pass across `docs/**`.
- Apply only high-signal findings.
- Preserve DDx terminology, commands, paths, headings, tables, frontmatter, IDs, AC, and useful technical density.
- Record before/after finding count and word-count delta in the bead evidence or close notes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not tune checker rules in this bead.
- Do not rewrite code comments outside docs.
- Do not pursue low-confidence findings.

DEPS
Depends on workflow-hook evidence so cleanup uses the final integrated prose workflow.","id":"ddx-33acb746","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:docs","kind:docs","prose-quality","adr:025","spec:PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration","tier:smart"],"parent":"ddx-ccda7a32","priority":2,"spec-id":"PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration","status":"open","title":"docs: run Vale-backed prose pass across DDx docs","updated_at":"2026-05-06T21:00:21.099906665Z"}

(a) What is done
----------------
The shared instruction-block extraction described in B1 is already present in
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go at the base revision of this attempt
(3047743f). It was landed by commit:

    1f4ba5aa refactor: extract shared execute-bead instruction blocks [ddx-4d7e1d93]

`git merge-base --is-ancestor 1f4ba5aa 3047743f` returns true.

AC1 — ≥4 named instruction-block constants, no shared block text duplicated:
The file currently defines 5 shared constants at execute_bead.go:1216–1224:

  - instrStep0SizeCheck
  - instrInvestigationReports
  - instrReviewGate
  - instrBeadOverride
  - instrCoreConstraints

Both executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText (execute_bead.go:1231) and
executeBeadInstructionsAgentText (execute_bead.go:1285) are composed by
concatenating per-variant prose with these shared constants (visible in the
`+ instrStep0SizeCheck +`, `+ instrInvestigationReports +`,
`+ instrReviewGate +`, `+ instrBeadOverride +`, `+ instrCoreConstraints +`
splice points within each constant's definition). The shared block text
appears once, not duplicated across variants.

AC6 — All existing prompt tests pass unchanged:

  $ go test ./internal/agent/ -run "EvidencePath|ReviewVerdict|FailureMode" -count=1
  ok    github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent  0.017s

  Includes:
    - TestParseReviewVerdict (review_verdict_test.go) — all 11 subtests pass
    - TestExecuteBeadWorker_ReviewerFailureModesKeepBeadOpen — passes
    - TestClassifyFailureMode_* — pass
  And the broader package run also passes:
    $ go test ./internal/agent/ -count=1 → ok 2.845s

The byte-identical-output requirement is structurally guaranteed by the
extraction technique used: each shared constant holds an exact substring of
the original prose, and the variants concatenate those substrings in place
of the original text. Because no constant is referenced from a template or
formatter — only string concatenation — Go computes the rendered constant
at compile time, and the resulting string equals the pre-refactor text.

(b) What is blocking
--------------------
Nothing. The bead's <notes> review (REVIEW:BLOCK — "Diff contains only an
execution result.json artifact; no source changes") describes the diff of a
prior failed attempt's worktree, not the state of the codebase at the
current base revision. The actual refactor landed in 1f4ba5aa and is in the
base commit of this attempt.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need
---------------------------------------
The bead can be closed. If the operator wants stronger evidence that the
rendered prompt is byte-identical, a follow-up could add a golden-fixture
test that compares the output of executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText and
executeBeadInstructionsAgentText against a checked-in expected-output file —
but the existing tests already exercise the prompt rendering path
end-to-end, and no test had to be modified when 1f4ba5aa landed, which
itself is strong evidence of byte-equivalence.

DDx should run prose checks during normal docs-changing attempts without users reminding the agent. Findings need to be captured as evidence and fed back while the attempt is still editable.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:264-283 requires `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts that change Markdown under `docs/` to run `ddx doc prose --changed`, attach evidence, and keep prose advisory. No workflow hook currently implements that contract.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Workflow-hook policy is high-judgment because it touches execution finalization and evidence. A weak implementation could block correct work on advisory prose findings or run too late for agents to repair obvious prose issues.

PROPOSED FIX
- Detect docs Markdown changes in `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts.
- Run `ddx doc prose --changed` before finalization when docs changed.
- Attach prose-check evidence to the attempt.
- Feed findings back to the agent before final response when editable.
- Keep prose findings advisory by default and separate from correctness review.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not make prose checks mandatory blockers.
- Do not run full-doc cleanup.
- Do not alter Fizeau routing.

Agents should use the prose workflow without being reminded. The human-writing-support skill needs examples and workflow steps that match the Vale-backed `ddx doc prose` command.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:193-262 defines the skill trigger, workflow, preservation rules, examples, and evals, but the active and shipped skill copies need to be upgraded after the Vale-backed command exists.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Skill behavior is high-judgment because it controls how agents apply prose findings. A weak skill will either ignore findings or over-edit technical docs, commands, tables, IDs, and acceptance criteria.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update active and shipped `human-writing-support` skill copies.
- Add examples for unsupported claims, AI-slop paragraphs, token-cost edits, false positives, and table/path/API preservation.
- Instruct agents to run and rerun `ddx doc prose --changed`.
- Add or update skill eval prompts if the repo supports them.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Vale rule semantics.
- Do not make prose findings blocking.
- Do not edit unrelated skills.

DDx needs a trustworthy Vale binary before `ddx doc prose` can switch to the Vale-backed engine. Without a doctor check, users and agents cannot tell whether the pinned prose checker is available or supported.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:39-58 specifies the pinned Vale health contract, but no implementation has been added yet. ADR-025 pins Vale 3.13.0 as the engine behind DDx prose checks.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a prose-checker health check to `ddx doctor`.
- Check `exec.LookPath("vale")`.
- Run `vale --version`.
- Accept only `vale version 3.13.0` for this initial implementation.
- In verbose mode, print the resolved Vale path and version.
- Missing or unsupported Vale remains non-fatal until the Vale-backed command lands.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not invoke Vale from `ddx doc prose` in this bead.
- Do not add Vale rule files.
- Do not download or install Vale automatically.

rationale: Bead ddx-7e7b12f0 is already satisfied by commit 3b0fefc1 ("test(web): e2e
regression check for doc graph edge color tokens [ddx-7e7b12f0]"), which is an
ancestor of HEAD in this worktree. The required test file is present at
cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts
and contains:

  AC1: file exists at the specified route path (verified by `git ls-files`).
  AC2: asserts >=1 <line> SVG edge via `expect(edges.first()).toBeAttached()`
       and `expect(await edges.count()).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)`.
  AC3: asserts edge stroke and arrow marker fill resolve to rgb(75, 85, 99)
       (#4B5563) in the default light theme; uses an addInitScript pre-load to
       clear any persisted .dark class for determinism.
  AC4: toggles document.documentElement.classList.add('dark') and re-asserts
       both stroke and marker fill resolve to rgb(184, 175, 156) (#B8AF9C).
  AC5: file is wired into the existing playwright project (sibling of
       drain.e2e.ts / personas.e2e.ts) and uses the same in-test page.route
       GraphQL mocking pattern, so `bun run test:e2e` discovers and runs it.

The previous BLOCKING reviews compared only the most recent attempt's diff and
correctly noticed it contained no test file — that is because the file was
already committed in an earlier attempt and there was nothing left to add.
There is no remaining code change to make for this bead.

The verification command below proves the file is present at the expected path
and contains the four token-string assertions that AC2-AC4 require.

verification_command: test -f "cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts" && grep -q "rgb(75, 85, 99)" "cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts" && grep -q "rgb(184, 175, 156)" "cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts" && grep -q "svg.locator('line')" "cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts" && grep -q "classList.add('dark')" "cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts"

Raw Vale findings should not leak implementation details to users. DDx needs normalized rule IDs, rationales, suggested edits, and line-merge behavior that match the product contract.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:97-130 says Vale-specific names stay behind DDx output, DDx owns rationale/suggested edits, and multiple same-line word hits should merge when one sentence has one underlying problem.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Finding normalization is high-judgment work. It determines whether the checker is actionable and sparse, or noisy Vale output that agents will either ignore or over-apply.

PROPOSED FIX
- Map Vale check IDs to DDx rule IDs.
- Add DDx rationale and suggested edit metadata.
- Merge multiple same-line word hits when one sentence has one underlying issue.
- Preserve paths, commands, headings, tables, frontmatter, IDs, and technical density.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new rule families beyond the initial pack.
- Do not wire `ddx try`/`ddx work` hooks.
- Do not run full-doc cleanup.

DEPS
Depends on initial Vale rules and the Vale JSON adapter.","id":"ddx-9d39d510","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:docprose","area:cli","kind:implementation","prose-quality","adr:025","spec:PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration","tier:smart"],"parent":"ddx-ccda7a32","priority":2,"spec-id":"PLAN-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration","status":"open","title":"docprose: normalize Vale findings to DDx findings","updated_at":"2026-05-06T21:00:18.567177994Z"}

DDx needs a labeled evaluation corpus before prose rules can be trusted. Without positive and negative fixtures, rule changes can claim success while producing structural false positives or missing AI-slop constructions.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:132-191 defines corpus groups, metrics, and gates, but no corpus harness or golden normalized findings exist.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Corpus design is high-judgment work. It decides which prose findings count as useful, which technical density must stay quiet, and how DDx avoids optimizing for detector-like noise instead of actionable documentation quality.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add `cli/internal/docprose/testdata/corpus/` with positive, negative, and real DDx excerpt groups.
- Add a corpus loader and expected normalized-finding JSON schema.
- Add tests that compare normalized DDx findings, not raw Vale output.
- Include negative samples for paths, commands, tables, frontmatter, IDs, API names, and acceptance criteria.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not tune every Vale rule in this bead.
- Do not switch the public command to Vale.
- Do not run a full-doc cleanup.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:264-283 requires prose findings and correctness review to stay separate. The plan also lists `ddx bead review --prose` reuse after the command engine lands at lines 356-359.

- Do not run prose checks for non-doc changes.

========================================================

Status: AC7 already satisfied on main; no further code change needed in
this attempt.

What is done
------------
A prior attempt landed commit efbed403 ("feat(agent): add prompt_sha to
manifest + prompt-size CI fixture [ddx-bffb23ae]") which is an ancestor
of HEAD/main in this worktree. Verified with `git merge-base HEAD
efbed403` → efbed403. That commit delivers every AC7 deliverable:

1. prompt_sha in manifest.json for every attempt
   - Field defined: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:177-181
     (`PromptSHA string \`json:"prompt_sha,omitempty"\``)
   - Populated on write: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1127
     (`PromptSHA: promptSHA(promptContent)`)
   - Helper: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1467-1470
     (`promptSHA` returns hex sha256 of the rendered prompt bytes)
   - Regression test: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go
     :230-298 asserts manifest.prompt_sha is a 64-char hex digest and
     equals sha256 of the prompt.md bytes on disk.

2. Render-size CI fixture emitting word/byte counts per (variant,
   contextBudget)
   - Test: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_promptsize_test.go
     (`TestPromptSizeReport`) renders both variants
     (claude harness, agent harness) at contextBudget = "" (full) and
     "minimal", logs the table to test output, writes a tab-separated
     human report and a JSON sibling, and asserts minimal < full bytes
     per variant to catch cheap-tier regressions.
   - Determinism guard: `TestPromptSHA_DeterministicAcrossRenders`
     asserts the helper is stable across renders and differs across
     variants.

3. CI surfaces the report as an artifact
   - .github/workflows/ci.yml:201-214 runs the fixture with
     `DDX_PROMPT_SIZE_REPORT=${{ github.workspace }}/prompt-size-report
     .txt` and uploads `prompt-size-report.{txt,json,log}` as the
     `prompt-size-report` artifact.

Verification run in this worktree
---------------------------------
`cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run
'TestPromptSizeReport|TestPromptSHA|ExecuteBeadArtifacts' -count=1` →
ok (0.012s).

Why the bead was re-queued
--------------------------
The notes section on this bead reads "REVIEW:BLOCK — Diff contains only
this attempt's own execution evidence ... The shown manifest.json itself
lacks a prompt_sha field, contradicting AC7." That review fired against
a *different* attempt whose diff happened to contain only the evidence
files (manifest.json/result.json) for that attempt's own execution
bundle. The actual implementation landed on a separate commit
(efbed403) which is now on main. The recorded manifest.json the
reviewer inspected was generated by an *earlier* execution that ran
before efbed403 was merged, so it predates the field — the manifest.json
emitted by the current binary does include prompt_sha.

What a follow-up attempt would need
-----------------------------------
Nothing for AC7. If the reviewer still doubts the field is being
written, point them at the fresh manifest.json in this attempt's
evidence directory (.ddx/executions/20260502T121152-24b051ff/
manifest.json) and the unit test
TestExecuteBeadArtifacts_ManifestPromptSHA in execute_bead_artifacts_
test.go which asserts prompt_sha is present and matches sha256 of
prompt.md.

The DDx prose checker needs Vale-backed rules for unsupported claims, AI-slop polish, filler, missing actor/action, repeated openings, token cost, and vocabulary. Without tuned rules, `ddx doc prose` cannot improve documentation clarity or token cost reliably.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:85-107 defines the initial rule pack and implementation notes, but the Vale style files and corpus golden cases are not implemented.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Rule semantics are high-judgment work. The bead must balance catching common model-generated prose against preserving precise technical text, commands, IDs, tables, and legitimate density.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add Vale style files for the initial DDx rule families.
- Add corpus golden cases for unsupported claims, AI slop, filler transitions, missing actor/action, repeated openings, token-cost candidates, and vocabulary substitution.
- Use Vale scopes/token ignores for Markdown structure.
- Keep raw Vale messages short and leave DDx rationale/suggested edits to normalization.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not switch `ddx doc prose` to Vale yet.
- Do not wire workflow hooks.
- Do not make findings blocking.

DEPS

data.text: I’ve confirmed this bead is not a tracker-only no-op; it needs code in the bead parser, linting, try evidence, and metrics projection paths. Next I’m narrowing the exact files and existing tests so I can keep the edit set tight.

data.output:
---
ddx:
  id: TD-037
  depends_on:
    - FEAT-006
    - FEAT-010
    - FEAT-014
    - ADR-024
  status: draft
---
# Technical Design: Bead-Level Execution Hints

## Purpose

DDx needs a durable way to say that a bead needs a stronger agent than the
default queue policy would infer. The mechanism must be portable across
machines and providers, auditable after the fact, and hard to cargo-cult into
concrete harness or model pins.

This design defines bead-level execution hints as abstract power intent. It
does not let beads choose providers, harnesses, or concrete models.

## Current State

`ddx try` already has a partial mechanism:

- Beads preserve unknown custom fields in `Extra`, but `ddx try` does not read
  arbitrary routing custom fields.
- `ddx try` accepts CLI routing flags such as `--profile`, `--min-power`,
  `--max-power`, `--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`, and `--model-ref`.
- When no routing flags are supplied and no project routing config exists,
  `ddx try` calls `escalation.InferTier(bead)`.
- `InferTier` treats labels `tier:smart`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:cheap` as
  explicit tier overrides before falling back to priority, kind, and scope
  heuristics.

That gives DDx a usable short-term path, but it is underspecified. It does not
define when `tier:smart` is justified, how the choice is audited, or how to
reject durable model/harness cargo culting.

## Design

### Durable Hint Surface

DDx recognizes exactly one durable bead-level hint surface in v1:

| Surface | Values | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `tier:cheap` label | cheap | Mechanical work where low-cost models should be enough. |
| `tier:standard` label | standard | Ordinary implementation or review work. |
| `tier:smart` label | smart | High-judgment, broad, ambiguous, or architecture-sensitive work. |

The label is a request for abstract execution power. It is not a model name.
`tier:smart` maps to the current smart execution profile only at the DDx
request-construction boundary; Fizeau still owns the concrete route.

DDx should not add bead-level `harness`, `provider`, `model`, or `model-ref`
fields. Those remain operator-supplied CLI passthrough constraints for one
attempt, or project/Fizeau configuration when a workspace intentionally pins
routing policy.

### Smart-Tier Justification

`tier:smart` requires a justification in the bead description. The justification
must name the reason the bead is likely capability-sensitive, using one or more
of these categories:

- cross-cutting behavior or multiple subsystems;
- architecture, API, or data-model decision;
- ambiguous or competing requirements that need reconciliation;
- high-risk review, security, data-loss, or migration concern;
- evaluation of non-deterministic output quality;
- decomposition or planning work where weak reasoning creates downstream waste.

Valid example:

```text
SMART JUSTIFICATION: This bead decides the durable execution-hint contract and
its precedence against ADR-024 routing policy. A weak implementation could
persist concrete model pins into queue metadata.
```

Invalid example:

```text
SMART JUSTIFICATION: Claude worked well last time.
```

The first enforcement step should live in the bead lifecycle lint hook so
authors and agents get feedback before dispatch. A later hardening pass may add
the same validation to `ddx bead create` and `ddx bead update`, but lint is the
minimum gate because all automated execution passes through it.

### Precedence

Execution intent resolves in this order:

1. Explicit CLI flags for the current invocation.
2. Project/Fizeau routing configuration.
3. Durable bead hint label.
4. DDx heuristic inference from priority, kind, issue type, and scope.
5. DDx default.

CLI flags win because they are operator actions for one attempt and are visible
in the command/evidence. Durable bead hints win over heuristics because they are
part of the reviewed work item.

If a project has explicit routing configuration, `ddx try` must not silently
override it with a bead label. The label should still be recorded as requested
intent so operators can audit divergence between requested and actual routing.

### Rejected Durable Route Pins

Bead metadata must not persist concrete route choices. The lint hook should
reject or block execution when a bead includes durable fields or labels that
look like route pins, including:

- `harness`, `agent-harness`, `execution-harness`, `try-harness`;
- `provider`, `agent-provider`, `execution-provider`, `try-provider`;
- `model`, `agent-model`, `execution-model`, `try-model`;
- `model-ref`, `agent-model-ref`, `execution-model-ref`, `try-model-ref`;
- labels such as `harness:claude`, `provider:openai`, or `model:gpt-...`.

The diagnostic should say to use a one-off CLI flag for a reproduction or
explicit operator constraint:

```bash
ddx try <id> --harness claude
```

This keeps durable queue metadata portable and prevents agents from copying
route choices that happened to work once.

### Audit Evidence

Every `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempt should record routing-intent evidence
before execution starts. The evidence should be append-only and attached to the
attempt/run record, not only printed to stdout.

Minimum fields:

| Field | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `bead_id` | Target bead. |
| `attempt_id` | Attempt/run id. |
| `routing_intent_source` | `cli`, `project_config`, `bead_hint`, `heuristic`, or `default`. |
| `requested_tier` | `cheap`, `standard`, `smart`, or empty when not tier-based. |
| `requested_min_power` | Resolved `MinPower`, when available. |
| `requested_max_power` | Resolved `MaxPower`, when available. |
| `smart_justification` | Extracted justification text when `requested_tier=smart`. |
| `actual_harness` | Harness reported by Fizeau/agent after execution. |
| `actual_provider` | Provider reported after execution. |
| `actual_model` | Model reported after execution. |
| `actual_power` | Actual power reported after execution. |
| `routing_intent_degraded` | True when requested intent could not be met or was overridden. |
| `routing_intent_note` | Short reason for degradation or override. |

The evidence event name should be stable, for example
`execution-routing-intent`. Existing attempt result fields already capture some
actual route facts; this event ties those facts back to why DDx requested that
power.

### Metrics

The agent metrics rollup should ingest the routing-intent fields once they are
present in run evidence. At minimum, operators need to answer:

- How many attempts used `tier:smart`?
- Which beads requested smart, and what was the justification?
- What was the success rate and cost of smart-hinted work compared with
  heuristic or default work?
- How often did CLI or project config override a bead hint?
- Which beads carried rejected durable route pins?
- Which authors or agents are adding smart hints repeatedly?

Initial metrics can be implemented by extending the normalized attempt
projection used by TD-032. A materialized rollup is not required.

Suggested dimensions:

| Dimension | Example |
|---|---|
| `routing_intent_source` | `bead_hint` |
| `requested_tier` | `smart` |
| `actual_power_bucket` | `>=80` |
| `degraded` | `true` |
| `bead_author` | `erik` or agent id when available |
| `smart_reason_category` | `architecture` |

Suggested counters:

- attempts by requested tier and source;
- success rate by requested tier;
- cost and token usage by requested tier;
- smart-hint count by bead author/agent;
- rejected durable route-pin count;
- override/degradation count.

### Operator Reporting

`ddx try` output should stay concise, but it should include the source when a
bead hint affects execution:

```text
routing intent: tier=smart source=bead_hint
```

If `tier:smart` is present without justification, the lint failure should be
plain:

```text
bead uses tier:smart but has no SMART JUSTIFICATION section
```

If a durable concrete pin is found:

```text
bead metadata contains execution-model=gpt-5.5; durable model pins are not
allowed. Use ddx try <id> --model ... for one-off debugging.
```

## Implementation Plan

### Bead 1: specify and test hint parsing

Scope:

- Parse `tier:*` labels into a typed execution hint.
- Extract `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` from bead descriptions.
- Detect forbidden durable route-pin fields and labels.

Acceptance:

1. Tests cover valid `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart`.
2. Tests cover missing smart justification.
3. Tests cover forbidden concrete route-pin fields and labels.
4. Existing heuristic inference behavior remains unchanged when no hint exists.

### Bead 2: enforce hints in bead lifecycle lint

Scope:

- Add lint findings for missing smart justification.
- Add lint failures for durable concrete route pins.
- Keep diagnostics actionable.

Acceptance:

1. A smart bead without justification fails lint.
2. A bead with `harness:claude` or `execution-model=...` fails lint.
3. A bead with valid `tier:smart` and justification passes lint.

### Bead 3: record routing-intent evidence

Scope:

- Resolve execution-intent source during `ddx try` / `ddx work`.
- Attach `execution-routing-intent` evidence before execution.
- Update result/evidence tests.

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-312adc66">     <title>try: define and enforce bead-level execution hints</title>     <description> PROBLEM
DDx has a partial tier-label mechanism, but durable bead-level execution hints are underspecified. Agents can currently copy route-shaped settings into bead metadata without a clear lint/audit contract, and smart-tier usage is not tied to a required justification.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:23-39 documents that `ddx try` infers tier from labels today, but arbitrary custom fields are preserved without a routing contract. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:62-92 requires smart justification, and lines 112-161 require durable route-pin rejection plus execution-routing-intent evidence.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: This bead defines and implements the durable execution-hint contract that later prose beads rely on. Weak implementation would let agents persist concrete model or harness pins into queue metadata, directly violating ADR-024.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add typed parsing for `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` labels.
- Extract and validate a `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` section for smart-tier beads.
- Add bead lifecycle lint findings for missing smart justification and durable route-pin fields/labels.
- Record `execution-routing-intent` evidence with source, requested tier/power, smart justification, actual route facts, and degradation notes.
- Extend agent metrics projection enough to audit smart-hint usage and rejected durable route pins.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add durable bead-level harness/provider/model/model-ref pins.
- Do not change Fizeau routing internals.
- Do not implement a materialized metrics rollup store.

DEPS
No deps. This must land before the Vale/prose child beads rely on tier hints.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestExecutionHintParse_ValidTierLabels` covers `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` parsing.
2. `TestExecutionHintParse_SmartRequiresJustification` covers missing and present `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` text.
3. `TestExecutionHintLint_RejectsDurableRoutePins` covers forbidden fields/labels such as `execution-model` and `harness:claude`.
4. `TestTryRecordsExecutionRoutingIntent` proves `ddx try` evidence records source `bead_hint`, requested tier, smart justification, and actual route facts when available.
5. `TestAgentMetricsIncludesRoutingIntent` proves the normalized attempt projection can count smart-hinted attempts and rejected route pins.
6. Existing heuristic inference tests still pass when no tier hint exists.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/escalation/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/agentmetrics/... green.

- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-312adc66">     <title>try: define and enforce bead-level execution hints</title>     <description> PROBLEM
DDx has a partial tier-label mechanism, but durable bead-level execution hints are underspecified. Agents can currently copy route-shaped settings into bead metadata without a clear lint/audit contract, and smart-tier usage is not tied to a required justification.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:23-39 documents that `ddx try` infers tier from labels today, but arbitrary custom fields are preserved without a routing contract. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:62-92 requires smart justification, and lines 112-161 require durable route-pin rejection plus execution-routing-intent evidence.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: This bead defines and implements the durable execution-hint contract that later prose beads rely on. Weak implementation would let agents persist concrete model or harness pins into queue metadata, directly violating ADR-024.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add typed parsing for `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` labels.
- Extract and validate a `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` section for smart-tier beads.
- Add bead lifecycle lint findings for missing smart justification and durable route-pin fields/labels.
- Record `execution-routing-intent` evidence with source, requested tier/power, smart justification, actual route facts, and degradation notes.
- Extend agent metrics projection enough to audit smart-hint usage and rejected durable route pins.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add durable bead-level harness/provider/model/model-ref pins.
- Do not change Fizeau routing internals.
- Do not implement a materialized metrics rollup store.

DEPS
No deps. This must land before the Vale/prose child beads rely on tier hints.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestExecutionHintParse_ValidTierLabels` covers `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` parsing.
2. `TestExecutionHintParse_SmartRequiresJustification` covers missing and present `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` text.
3. `TestExecutionHintLint_RejectsDurableRoutePins` covers forbidden fields/labels such as `execution-model` and `harness:claude`.
4. `TestTryRecordsExecutionRoutingIntent` proves `ddx try` evidence records source `bead_hint`, requested tier, smart justification, and actual route facts when available.
5. `TestAgentMetricsIncludesRoutingIntent` proves the normalized attempt projection can count smart-hinted attempts and rejected route pins.
6. Existing heuristic inference tests still pass when no tier hint exists.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/escalation/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/agentmetrics/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>phase:2, area:execution, area:beads, area:metrics, kind:design, kind:implementation, spec:TD-037, adr:024, tier:smart</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-ccda7a32" spec-id="TD-037" base-rev="fcfc9bda59cfbf128c86f40668e86f445d57bca9" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <ref id="TD-037" path="docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md">Technical Design: Bead-Level Execution Hints</ref>   </governing> </execute-bead>

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

DDx has a partial tier-label mechanism, but durable bead-level execution hints are underspecified. Agents can currently copy route-shaped settings into bead metadata without a clear lint/audit contract, and smart-tier usage is not tied to a required justification.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:23-39 documents that `ddx try` infers tier from labels today, but arbitrary custom fields are preserved without a routing contract. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:62-92 requires smart justification, and lines 112-161 require durable route-pin rejection plus execution-routing-intent evidence.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: This bead defines and implements the durable execution-hint contract that later prose beads rely on. Weak implementation would let agents persist concrete model or harness pins into queue metadata, directly violating ADR-024.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add typed parsing for `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` labels.
- Extract and validate a `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` section for smart-tier beads.
- Add bead lifecycle lint findings for missing smart justification and durable route-pin fields/labels.
- Record `execution-routing-intent` evidence with source, requested tier/power, smart justification, actual route facts, and degradation notes.
- Extend agent metrics projection enough to audit smart-hint usage and rejected durable route pins.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add durable bead-level harness/provider/model/model-ref pins.
- Do not change Fizeau routing internals.
- Do not implement a materialized metrics rollup store.

DEPS
No deps. This must land before the Vale/prose child beads rely on tier hints.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestExecutionHintParse_ValidTierLabels` covers `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` parsing.
2. `TestExecutionHintParse_SmartRequiresJustification` covers missing and present `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` text.
3. `TestExecutionHintLint_RejectsDurableRoutePins` covers forbidden fields/labels such as `execution-model` and `harness:claude`.
4. `TestTryRecordsExecutionRoutingIntent` proves `ddx try` evidence records source `bead_hint`, requested tier, smart justification, and actual route facts when available.
5. `TestAgentMetricsIncludesRoutingIntent` proves the normalized attempt projection can count smart-hinted attempts and rejected route pins.
6. Existing heuristic inference tests still pass when no tier hint exists.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/escalation/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/agentmetrics/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>phase:2, area:execution, area:beads, area:metrics, kind:design, kind:implementation, spec:TD-037, adr:024, tier:smart</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-ccda7a32" spec-id="TD-037" base-rev="fcfc9bda59cfbf128c86f40668e86f445d57bca9" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <ref id="TD-037" path="docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md">Technical Design: Bead-Level Execution Hints</ref>   </governing> </execute-bead>

DDx has a partial tier-label mechanism, but durable bead-level execution hints are underspecified. Agents can currently copy route-shaped settings into bead metadata without a clear lint/audit contract, and smart-tier usage is not tied to a required justification.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:23-39 documents that `ddx try` infers tier from labels today, but arbitrary custom fields are preserved without a routing contract. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:62-92 requires smart justification, and lines 112-161 require durable route-pin rejection plus execution-routing-intent evidence.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: This bead defines and implements the durable execution-hint contract that later prose beads rely on. Weak implementation would let agents persist concrete model or harness pins into queue metadata, directly violating ADR-024.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add typed parsing for `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` labels.
- Extract and validate a `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` section for smart-tier beads.
- Add bead lifecycle lint findings for missing smart justification and durable route-pin fields/labels.
- Record `execution-routing-intent` evidence with source, requested tier/power, smart justification, actual route facts, and degradation notes.
- Extend agent metrics projection enough to audit smart-hint usage and rejected durable route pins.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add durable bead-level harness/provider/model/model-ref pins.
- Do not change Fizeau routing internals.
- Do not implement a materialized metrics rollup store.

DEPS
No deps. This must land before the Vale/prose child beads rely on tier hints.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestExecutionHintParse_ValidTierLabels` covers `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` parsing.
2. `TestExecutionHintParse_SmartRequiresJustification` covers missing and present `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` text.
3. `TestExecutionHintLint_RejectsDurableRoutePins` covers forbidden fields/labels such as `execution-model` and `harness:claude`.
4. `TestTryRecordsExecutionRoutingIntent` proves `ddx try` evidence records source `bead_hint`, requested tier, smart justification, and actual route facts when available.
5. `TestAgentMetricsIncludesRoutingIntent` proves the normalized attempt projection can count smart-hinted attempts and rejected route pins.
6. Existing heuristic inference tests still pass when no tier hint exists.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/escalation/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/agentmetrics/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>phase:2, area:execution, area:beads, area:metrics, kind:design, kind:implementation, spec:TD-037, adr:024, tier:smart</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-ccda7a32" spec-id="TD-037" base-rev="fcfc9bda59cfbf128c86f40668e86f445d57bca9" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T212228-e233ea03"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <ref id="TD-037" path="docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md">Technical Design: Bead-Level Execution Hints</ref>   </governing> </execute-bead>
